[Question]
What could be the possible causes of toothache?

There are several possible causes of toothache. Some common causes include:

- Tooth decay or cavities: Bacteria in the mouth can cause acid to build up on teeth, leading to decay and potentially causing pain.
- Gum disease: Inflammation of the gums (gingivitis) or more severe gum disease (periodontitis) can cause pain and discomfort in the teeth and surrounding tissues.
- Cracked or chipped teeth: Damage to the enamel or dentin of a tooth can expose sensitive nerves, leading to pain.
- Abscesses: Pus-filled pockets around the roots of teeth can cause severe pain and swelling.
- Loose fillings or crowns: If a filling or crown becomes loose, it can irritate the nerve and cause pain.
- Grinding or clenching teeth: Excessive force on the teeth can lead to wear and tear, causing pain and sensitivity.
- Sinus pressure: Sinus infections or allergies can cause referred pain in the teeth.
- Dental work: Recent dental procedures, such as getting a filling or having a tooth extracted, can cause temporary pain.

It's essential to consult a dentist to determine the underlying cause of your toothache and receive proper treatment.
